title: Function parameter NULL-safety documented but not enforced
description:
Location: firmware/bridge-zephyr/subsys/lichen/lora_l2.h:157

lichen_lora_l2_get_eui64() docstring warns "Do NOT modify" but returns const uint8_t*. The const is correct but there is no runtime protection.

Also: lichen_lora_l2_set_rx_callback (line 523-534 in lora_l2.c) does not validate that cb is actually callable when non-NULL. If a caller passes a garbage pointer, it will be invoked in rx_thread.

What goes wrong: A corrupted callback pointer would crash in rx_thread context. While this is caller error, defensive NULL check before invocation is cheap and aids debugging.

Note: This is defense-in-depth, not a direct bug. Current code correctly updates both fields atomically under mutex.
